confirmed_task:
1. Navigate to https://elibrary.ferc.gov/eLibrary/search.
2. Ensure "General Search" is selected (it should be the default).
3. In the "Enter Docket Number" field, type CP23-29 exactly.
4. Leave "Date Range", "Select Category", and all other fields at their default values.
5. Click the blue "Search" button.
6. Wait for the "General Search Results" table to fully load.
7. Focus only on the first two data rows in the results table (Row 1 and Row 2).
8. For each of these two rows, read and record the text in the "Description" column.
9. In the same row, go to the "Files" column and identify ALL individual file links (for example, links to .pdf or other specific filenames). IMPORTANT: Ignore any "Generate PDF" link; do NOT click it. Only click the links that are specific filenames.
10. For each identified file link in that row, one by one:
    a. Click the filename link to open/download the document.
    b. Open the document and read its contents.
    c. Produce a concise textual summary of the document.
    d. Then go back (or switch tab) to return to the search results page, if needed, and proceed to the next file in the same row.
11. After all files for Row 1 are processed and summarized, repeat steps 8–10 for Row 2.
12. When finished, return the results as markdown text only (no file outputs), using exactly the following format for each file processed:

**[Row #] - [Docket Number]**
**File Name:** [Name of file]
**Description:** [Row Description]
**Summary:** [Concise summary of this specific file's contents]
--------------------------------------------------

13. If any documents are PDFs or other assets with direct URLs, include those URLs inline in the response next to the corresponding filename.
14. Do not save any information to local files; respond only with detailed markdown text as specified.

Navigate to the following URLs and perform the specified actions to extract telecom package data. For each site, extract: Provider, Package name, Offer type (New customer/Standard/etc), Price (DKK/month), Data/speed, Network type (5G/4G), Broadband type (Fixed/Mobile), Contract length, and Source URL.

1. https://www.samlino.dk/mobilabonnement?journeyId=398fb200-97e8-4efd-84e0-22b7ba058b73
   Action: Scroll below the 6th icon. Click "Vis mere" button. Wait 25s. Repeat until no more button. Capture all mobile packages.

2. https://www.samlino.dk/bredbaand/broadband
   Action: Scroll below the 6th icon. Click "Vis mere" button. Wait 25s. Repeat until no more button. Capture all broadband packages.
   Categorization Note: Fixed = "Fiber"/"Coax"/"kabel"; Mobile = "5G"/"4G"/"Mobilt bredbånd".

3. https://yousee.dk/shop/internet/coax
   Action: Enter address "Nørrebrogade 56, 2. TH, 2200 København N", select from dropdown, wait 10s. Extract available internet packages.

4. https://shop.norlys.dk/internet/produkter/?addressId=0a3f50b9-fa94-32b8-e044-0003ba298018&subscriptionId=FWABTC.FWABTCP
   Action: Extract the 2 fixed broadband prices shown under the 1000/1000 Mbit section.

5. https://www.3.dk/shop/mobilabonnementer/
   Extract all mobile packages.

Return a JSON-formatted list of all extracted packages.

Go to Facebook Ads Library at https://www.facebook.com/ads/library. Search for "eSIM" in the search box. Set the following filters: keep country/region as default or set to global if available, select all platforms, set ad status to "Active", and select all categories. Wait for results to load completely. Identify the 5 best performing ads based on longest active time or highest engagement metrics. For each of these 5 ads, collect the following information: 1) Take clear screenshots of the ad images/creatives, 2) Extract the advertiser name, 3) Extract the full ad copy text, 4) Record the deployment duration (how long the ad has been running), 5) Note which platforms the ad is deployed on (Facebook, Instagram, etc.). Take comprehensive screenshots of the search results page and detailed screenshots of each of the 5 selected ads. Return all extracted information in a structured format with advertiser names, ad copy, durations, and platform details clearly labeled.

Compare the prices for each of the SSDs (products) listed on "https://www.elgiganten.dk/gaming/hardware/intern-harddisk/intern-ssd" against the prices for the same SSDs (products) on "https://www.pricerunner.dk/". 

Output structure for each SSD:
<ssd_model> - <price_found_on_elgiganten.dk> (Elgiganten):
<bulletpoint_list>
  <cheapest_price> - <store_name> (<store_product_link>
  <second_cheapest_price> - <store_name> (<store_product_link>)
  <third_cheapest_price> - <store_name> (<store_product_link>)
</bulletpoint_list>

Example output:
Samsung 990 EVO Plus 2TB - 1.799 DKK (Elgiganten):
    * 1.799 DKK - Elgiganten (https://www.elgiganten.dk/product/gaming/pc-komponenter/intern-harddisk/intern-ssd/samsung-990-evo-plus-intern-ssd-2tb/895773)
    * 1.890 DKK - Scandinavian Photo (https://www.scandinavianphoto.dk/samsung/ssd-990-evo-plus-2tb-1102465)
    * 1.990 DKK - Proshop (https://www.proshop.dk/SSD/Samsung-990-EVO-Plus-SSD-2TB-M2-2280-PCIe-40/3303621)

Go to https://sbi.bank.in/web/interest-rates/interest-rates/loan-schemes-interest-rates/home-loans-interest-rates-current and take a screenshot of the complete "Home & Home Related Loan Interest Rate" table showing all loan types and their interest rate ranges. Scroll if necessary to capture the entire table. Return the screenshot path.

Navigate to https://topplasticsurgeonreviews.com/beverlyhills/ and extract the full list of plastic surgeons, their names, and ratings. Then navigate to https://find.plasticsurgery.org/?state=CA&city=Beverly%20Hills and extract as many names and practices as possible from the results (there are likely hundreds). Combine these lists and for each surgeon, identify which of the 13 specialties they offer. The specialties are: Breast Augmentation/Reconstruction, Rhinoplasty, Face Lift, Facial Reconstruction, Liposuction, Eyelid/Oculoplasty, Tummy Tuck, Mommy Makeover, Injectors/Botox/Fillers, Cosmetic/Anti-Aging, Brazilian Butt Lift (BBL), Cosmetic Laser, Hair Restoration. I need to reach 40 candidates per specialty. Ensure ABPS board certification is noted.

Open https://www.fcc.gov/oet/ea/fccid . For each of the following FCC grantee codes: 2ACAH, BCE, 2A3UL, X26, U28, 2AXDT, 2AS2V, perform these steps:
1. In the search form, enter the code into the "Grantee Code" field only (leave other fields blank).
2. Submit the search.
3. On the search results page, locate the text indicating the total number of matching results. On this FCC site it is typically phrased like "Matches 1 - 25 of 58" or similar. Extract the final total count number (e.g., 58 in that example).
4. Record for each grantee code: the grantee code, the total number of results, and the exact snippet of text used to infer the count.
5. If the total number of results is not clearly visible, attempt to infer it by inspecting pagination controls (e.g., last page number * 25 plus last page row count), and describe how you inferred it.
Return a concise JSON array, one object per grantee code, with fields: grantee_code, result_count (integer if possible, else null), evidence_text (string of the UI text you used to determine the count), and notes (short description of any ambiguity or calculation).

Go to https://www.wakemed.org/providers and paginate through all provider listing pages. For each doctor, open the doctor profile page and extract doctor data plus all visible metadata from inside each doctor profile. Return JSON with one object per doctor including profile_url and all extracted fields (name, credentials, specialties, locations, phones, languages, clinical interests, education/training, board certifications, and any other visible profile metadata). JSON only.
